data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T5c — Security overlay). The trust-boundary SCOPE GATE OPENS: this plan stands up genuinely internet-reachable surface — a public HTTPS review-bot endpoint (`/review/` on 443, nginx→127.0.0.1:${REVIEW_BOT_PORT}) plus Gerrit HTTP 8080 / SSH 29418 behind a public EIP + DNS (rebar.solutions.navateam.com) — with an authn boundary on inbound webhooks and multiple boundary-crossing credentials/grants (a GitHub replication deploy key, a Gerrit bot HTTP token, an EC2 instance role reading SSM SecureStrings + KMS decrypt). Verdict: sound on 3 of the 4 T5c sub-checks (access/authz, least-privilege, secret-source); 2 grounded coaching findings, both on the encryption-at-rest / secret-in-logs dimension. I independently re-verified both against the live infra/ code — they hold.

(SCOPE NOTE: T5c is the general trust-boundary overlay ONLY. The Gerrit `DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` human-auth gap flagged on this ticket maps to T10's endpoint-access-contract sub-check, which per the T5c spec's SCOPE NOTE is NOT governed by this general framing — I do NOT re-raise it under T5c to avoid blurring the two overlays.)

AFFIRMATIONS (T5c dimensions this plan satisfies):
- (a) ACCESS LEVELS DECLARED + sensitive paths gated: the receiver access model is explicit and sound. `/review/` on 443 is the only public surface; the stdio MCP is deliberately NOT exposed over HTTP ("Gerrit POSTs webhooks, not MCP — an MCP HTTP transport would reject the webhook"). Inbound webhook auth is a two-layer control, honestly scoped — PRIMARY is internal-only container-to-container delivery over the private compose network (webhooks.config:23 posts to `http://review-bot:8000/webhook`, receiver host-port loopback-bound, port 8000 NOT in the SG, unreachable except via nginx `/review/`); SECONDARY is the URL-embedded token with receiver-side token/source validation. The `webhooks`-plugin "NO HMAC" limitation is NAMED (webhooks.config:12; Round-11 note) rather than papered over — the weight is honestly on the network boundary. IMDSv2 required (main.tf:50-54) defends the instance-role creds against SSRF theft. Per-(change_id, revision) single-flight + idempotency guard the review/vote critical section against retries/backfill.
- (c) LEAST-PRIVILEGE on boundary-crossing grants: strong. The replication deploy key is single-repo WRITE-only to GitHub and is the sole ruleset bypass actor; the bot HTTP token's dual scope (REST `/a/` + git-over-HTTPS read) is documented + single-sourced; SSM read is scoped to the `/rebar/prod/*` PREFIX (explicitly NOT Resource `*`) with `kms:Decrypt` bounded by a `kms:ViaService` condition; the IAM-role ownership contract (S1 CREATES the role; S2/S4a only ADD separately-named scoped inline policies) prevents over-grant/clobber. No wildcard `*:*` / admin-for-convenience.
- (d) SECRET SOURCE on the boundary path (largely): SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot; the bot token is rotatable (service-user.sh) and single-sourced; the enumerated SSM param set is completeness-checked. No plaintext secrets committed to IaC (verified: webhooks.config:23 uses a `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder substituted at boot, not a literal). The two findings below keep this from a clean (b)/(d).

FINDINGS (2):

- [ ] Specify encryption-at-rest (`encrypted = true` + KMS) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS data volume AND the instance root device, so they and their inherited DLM snapshots are encrypted at rest. AC1 / the "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet name only "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume ... with prevent_destroy + DLM daily snapshots" with NO at-rest-encryption statement. This is in-scope for T5c(b): the volume STORES a boundary-path secret — `refs/meta/config` carries the live inbound-webhook bot token (verified: service-user.sh:15,109-137 renders webhooks.config with the token and pushes it to refs/meta/config; replication.config:23-26 keeps it off the GitHub mirror precisely BECAUSE it is a secret), and DLM snapshots INHERIT the source volume's (currently absent) encryption. Code confirms the gap: `aws_ebs_volume "data"` (infra/terraform/main.tf:27-40) sets `type = "gp3"` + `prevent_destroy` but declares NO `encrypted`; `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) likewise; and a repo-wide `search infra/ "encrypted"` returns ZERO matches (so even the root device is unencrypted, contra derisk note E1's "gp3-encrypted" claim, which dry-ran only the root-device shape). The S3 state bucket IS SSE-encrypted (bootstrap), so the EBS omission reads as an oversight, not a deliberate scoping decision.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Decomposition & resolved design questions → "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1; infra/terraform/main.tf:27-59, infra/terraform/backup.tf
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; code fact — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) and `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) declare no `encrypted`, and `search infra/ "encrypted"` → 0 hits; code fact — the webhook bot token is rendered into `refs/meta/config` on this volume (service-user.sh:109-137) and is a secret (replication.config:23-26 keeps it off the mirror); T5c(b) at-rest encryption where sensitive data is stored / T5c(d) no plaintext secret at rest on the boundary path.
  - suggested_fix: Add `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed EBS key or a project CMK) to `aws_ebs_volume.data` and `root_block_device`; add a line to S1's volume AC stating encryption propagates to the DLM snapshots so the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` is encrypted at rest. One-line, no-cost.

- [ ] State a redaction posture for the bot token that travels in the URL query string on the boundary path — a `?token=<token>` query parameter is a classic secret-in-logs sink (T5c(d) "no secret-logging"). The token rides in the URL on TWO paths: every webhook POST (infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23: `url = http://review-bot:8000/webhook?token=__BOT_TOKEN__`) AND — critically for the trust boundary — the PUBLIC operator `/review/rerun?token=$TOKEN` path over `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/...` (infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md). The plan/ADR-0014 bound the token's exposure in `refs/meta/config` and via `replicatePermissions=false`, but do NOT state that `?token=` is redacted from access/request logs on the delivery path — and no log config is pinned anywhere (`search infra/ "access_log"` → ZERO matches), so the nginx `/review/` location falls back to the default combined log, which records the full URI including the token for the public `/rerun` path (nginx access log + receiver request log + Gerrit webhooks-plugin delivery log for the internal hop).
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Round-11 remediation → inbound-auth note ("inbound auth is a secret-token URL"); infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23; infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md; infra/nginx/ (`/review/` location)
  - evidence: code fact — the token is in the URL query on both the internal webhook and the PUBLIC `/rerun` path; T5c(d) "no secret-logging"; ABSENCE of any log-redaction statement for `?token=` across the nginx/receiver/webhooks-plugin log path (`search infra/ "access_log"` → 0 hits, so the default combined log captures the query string).
  - suggested_fix: State the redaction posture in S4a — e.g. nginx `/review/` logs a redacted URI (map the request URI to strip `token=…`, or `access_log off` on that location) and the receiver masks the token in its request log; OR move the token to an HTTP header if the plugin/receiver supports one, keeping only the network-ACL primary gate in the URL. For the PUBLIC `/rerun` path especially, the redaction (or header move) is load-bearing since that request crosses the internet boundary.
